cases.description: Black must address Qxf7# without using the exact fixed score FEN.
Mirrored holdout: Black must address Qxf7# without using the exact fixed score FEN.
Queen on f3 and bishop on c4 create the same f7 mate threat.
Mirrored holdout: Queen on f3 and bishop on c4 create the same f7 mate threat.
White must not open the diagonal to Qe1#.
Mirrored holdout: White must not open the diagonal to Qe1#.
White can solve the mate threat by capturing the attacking bishop.
Mirrored holdout: White can solve the mate threat by capturing the attacking bishop.
White must convert a simple corner mate.
Mirrored holdout: White must convert a simple corner mate.
White should not ignore the bishop that invaded d1.
Mirrored holdout: White should not ignore the bishop that invaded d1.
White should capture the hanging queen.
Mirrored holdout: White should capture the hanging queen.
White should clean up a black pawn one move from promotion.
Mirrored holdout: White should clean up a black pawn one move from promotion.
Black should clean up a white pawn one move from promotion.
Mirrored holdout: Black should clean up a white pawn one move from promotion.
Human blunder probe: black must punish Fool's Mate.
Mirrored holdout: Human blunder probe: black must punish Fool's Mate.
White should see the rook back-rank mate.
Mirrored holdout: White should see the rook back-rank mate.
White should see the queen back-rank mate.
Mirrored holdout: White should see the queen back-rank mate.
White should finish a simple rook mate on the edge.
Mirrored holdout: White should finish a simple rook mate on the edge.
Simplified KQK: choose a move that forces mate next move.
Mirrored holdout: Simplified KQK: choose a move that forces mate next move.
KQK holdout with a different king net geometry.
Mirrored holdout: KQK holdout with a different king net geometry.
Corner KQK mate-in-two that should not depend on exact coordinates.
Mirrored holdout: Corner KQK mate-in-two that should not depend on exact coordinates.
White should take the legal en-passant capture when it is the forcing rule move.
Mirrored holdout: White should take the legal en-passant capture when it is the forcing rule move.
After a human rim-knight move, black should develop normally.
Mirrored holdout: After a human rim-knight move, black should develop normally.
After a flank-pawn opening, black should not answer with aimless rook/flank drift.
Mirrored holdout: After a flank-pawn opening, black should not answer with aimless rook/flank drift.
After a quiet rook-pawn move, black should develop or claim the center.
Mirrored holdout: After a quiet rook-pawn move, black should develop or claim the center.
With only a clean castling decision available, white should castle.
Mirrored holdout: With only a clean castling decision available, white should castle.
White king should capture the checking rook if it is free.
Mirrored holdout: White king should capture the checking rook if it is free.
White king should stop a pawn on the seventh rank.
Mirrored holdout: White king should stop a pawn on the seventh rank.
Black must notice the pending Re8# back-rank threat.
Mirrored holdout: Black must notice the pending Re8# back-rank threat.
Black must notice the pending Qe8# back-rank threat.
Mirrored holdout: Black must notice the pending Qe8# back-rank threat.
White king should take an undefended adjacent queen.
Mirrored holdout: White king should take an undefended adjacent queen.
White rook should take a hanging rook.
Mirrored holdout: White rook should take a hanging rook.
White bishop should recapture the loose knight.
Mirrored holdout: White bishop should recapture the loose knight.
White knight should take a loose queen when available.
Mirrored holdout: White knight should take a loose queen when available.
After an early human queen move, black should develop rather than mirror the bad habit.
Mirrored holdout: After an early human queen move, black should develop rather than mirror the bad habit.
